oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object routed by operation) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature10_integrated_autolinking, feature11_emoji_detection_and_rendering, feature12_emoji_data_packaging, feature1_markup_wrapping, feature2_line_break_conversion, feature3_safe_html_sanitization, feature4_attribute_normalization, feature5_input_validation, feature6_email_detection, feature7_hashtag_detection, feature8_url_detection, feature9_ip_url_detection. Feature descriptions include: Render plain text with email, hashtag, host-name URL, and IPv4 URL tokens converted into anchor elements without nesting anchors inside existing anchors.; Detect configured emoji unicode, shortcode, and emoticon tokens and render matched emoji either as unicode or image markup.; Package raw emoji records into enriched emoji data with canonical shortcode, primary shortcode, unicode value, and skin metadata.; Render text or empty content through a safe markup renderer using the requested outer container behavior.; Convert plain-text line breaks into rendered break elements only when the input is plain text and conversion is enabled.; Render supported HTML fragments while removing disallowed elements and unsafe source attributes.; Render allowed HTML attributes in normalized form and reject unsafe or unsupported attributes.; Reject unsupported whole-document markup and non-text content with language-neutral error categories.; Detect supported email-address tokens and expose their local and host parts.; Detect supported hashtag tokens and expose the tag text without the leading marker.; Detect supported web URL tokens and expose their scheme, host, path, query, and fragment parts.; Detect supported IPv4 URL tokens and expose their scheme, host, path, query, port, and fragment parts.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: operation=['emoji_match', 'match_pattern', 'package_emoji', 'parse_content', 'render_autolinks', 'render_markup', 'validate_document_fragment'], content=['- https://127.0.0.1/foo - www.domain.com - (http://', '123', 'Foo', '

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_integrated_autolinking.json.description: Render plain text with email, hashtag, host-name URL, and IPv4 URL tokens converted into anchor elements without nesting anchors inside existing anchors.

input.content: - https://127.0.0.1/foo - www.domain.com - (http://domain.com/some/path?with=query) - This text should stay

cases.expected_output: - https://127.0.0.1/foo - www.domain.com - (http://domain.com/some/path?with=query) - This text should stay

feature11_emoji_detection_and_rendering..description: Detect configured emoji unicode, shortcode, and emoticon tokens and render matched emoji either as unicode or image markup.

feature12_emoji_data_packaging.json.description: Package raw emoji records into enriched emoji data with canonical shortcode, primary shortcode, unicode value, and skin metadata.

feature1_markup_wrapping.json.description: Render text or empty content through a safe markup renderer using the requested outer container behavior.

feature2_line_break_conversion.json.description: Convert plain-text line breaks into rendered break elements only when the input is plain text and conversion is enabled.

feature3_safe_html_sanitization.json.description: Render supported HTML fragments while removing disallowed elements and unsafe source attributes.

feature4_attribute_normalization.json.description: Render allowed HTML attributes in normalized form and reject unsafe or unsupported attributes.

feature5_input_validation.json.description: Reject unsupported whole-document markup and non-text content with language-neutral error categories.

feature6_email_detection.json.description: Detect supported email-address tokens and expose their local and host parts.

feature7_hashtag_detection.json.description: Detect supported hashtag tokens and expose the tag text without the leading marker.

feature8_url_detection.json.description: Detect supported web URL tokens and expose their scheme, host, path, query, and fragment parts.
